verdicts.rationale: slices/abapgit-standalone-demo/inputs/expert-answers/2026-07-03-owner-demo-context.md:19 (gap g001) states verbatim that the owner downloaded the abapGit standalone program from docs.abapgit.org on 2026-07-02 to demonstrate/benchmark the abap_wiki L0/L1/L2 ingest pipeline and that it is not part of any business process. Fully matches the sentence; no L1 contradiction (L1 3.1 independently frames the object as vendored infrastructure tooling, not ACME business logic).

slices/abapgit-standalone-demo/inputs/expert-answers/2026-07-03-owner-demo-context.md:23 (gap g005) states verbatim that no background/batch job is configured for zcl_abapgit_background=>run and the program is never executed here, used only as pipeline input. Matches the sentence directly. No contradiction with L1: the L1 page never claims the object was executed; L1 business open question 4 independently flags whether any background job is configured as unverifiable from source alone, which is consistent.

slices/abapgit-standalone-demo/research/2026-07-03-standalone-vs-developer-version.md:21-27 quotes the official docs verbatim: the standalone version 'is executed via transaction SE38', contrasted with the developer version which is 'Run via transaction ZABAPGIT'; lines 29-34 list the 5 install steps for the standalone version (download, create report via SE38/SE80/ADT, upload, activate, run via SE38) and none of the 5 steps creates a dedicated transaction code. This directly supports both halves of the sentence. Checked against L1 for contradiction: L1 section 3.4 ('Known caller') contains a hedged, uncited, explicitly [UNVERIFIABLE] remark that 'this program is normally started directly via SE38/its own transaction code' -- this is generic unevidenced speculation by the L1 author, not a code-based fact, so it does not rise to a code-proven contradiction of the research-sourced claim.

slices/abapgit-standalone-demo/research/2026-07-03-online-vs-offline-modes.md:11-17 and :19-26 describe the online (HTTP-based, IF_HTTP_CLIENT/CL_HTTP_CLIENT=>CREATE_BY_URL) and offline (ZIP-based, CL_ABAP_ZIP) paths and explicitly tie both to dependencies documented in the L1 page for ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE. Consistent with L1 section 8.3 Classes, which independently lists CL_ABAP_ZIP ('ZIP packaging for export/offline transport') and IF_HTTP_CLIENT ('outbound HTTP(S) to git remotes') as unconditional dependencies of the object -- no contradiction, and the code evidence reinforces that both paths are structurally present, not user-configured additions.

slices/abapgit-standalone-demo/inputs/expert-answers/2026-07-03-owner-demo-context.md:31 (gap g007) states verbatim that the ZABAPGIT package and its single-row TADIR are synthetic, generated by the demo harness purely to exercise the pipeline's package layout, and that the deviation is an intentional demo fixture, not a transport-governance decision. Matches the sentence exactly. No contradiction with L1 (L1 frontmatter independently confirms devclass ZABAPGIT / namespace Z / custom package, a fact about placement, not about intent, so it cannot and does not contradict the intent clarification).

Combines two cited passages: owner-demo-context.md:21-23 (gap g005) states the program is never executed here (covering the interactive/background execution-mode axis), and owner-demo-context.md:33-35 (gap g009) states verbatim 'Neither online nor offline mode is used in this context: the program is never run' (covering the transport-mode axis). Together they fully support the combined sentence. No contradiction with L1 (L1 3.2 documents both modes existing in the code but never claims either was exercised in this workspace).

Class BUSINESS-RULE, status not-verifiable, evidence intentionally empty -- per gate rule 6 this class is capped at partially_supported regardless of reasonableness. The characterization is fully consistent with the record: slices/abapgit-standalone-demo/gaps.yaml gap abapgit-standalone-demo-g006 (status: asked, class: BUSINESS-RULE) asks exactly this question and is unresolved, and it maps 1:1 onto BUG-001 in the L1 page (wiki/ZABAPGIT/program-ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE.md, Bug candidates table and section 10.2/10.3: 'COMMIT WORK once per deleted object inside LOOP AT lt_tadir ... no batching and no explicit partial-completion handling'). No contradiction found.

Class BUSINESS-RULE, status not-verifiable, evidence intentionally empty -- per gate rule 6 this class is capped at partially_supported regardless of reasonableness. Fully consistent with slices/abapgit-standalone-demo/gaps.yaml gap abapgit-standalone-demo-g011 (status: asked, class: BUSINESS-RULE, description matches verbatim) and with the L1 page's own open question 3 and section 7.2 ('what the emergency DB-utility mode (DBT = 'ZABAPGIT') actually exposes was not traced further in this pass'). No contradiction found.
